title: Nutty Crisp Philippine Cassava Stir-fry
description: A diabetic-friendly Filipino dish featuring crispy cassava with a nutty twist, made in one pot for a quick and easy dinner option.
instructions: Cut cassava into thin slices, about ¼ inch thick.
Season both sides of the cassava with salt and pepper.
Mix tahini sauce with a little water until smooth.
Place cassava slices on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Drizzle with some oil and brush evenly across all slices.
Bake for 20-25 minutes or until golden brown and crispy.
While cassava is baking, sauté garlic in a little bit of oil until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add red bell pepper to the pan and cook for another 3-4 minutes until slightly softened.
Combine baked cassava with sautéed vegetables and tahini sauce. Mix well.
Sprinkle sesame seeds over the top just before serving.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course.
